name: fe1_fresh_right_flagged_left
drl:
rule TJ1 salience 5 when $a : E0() from entry-point "S1" $b : E2($t : tag, this before[0ms,50ms] $a) then insertLogical(new D($t)); end
rule RN salience 9 when not D() P() then end
rule K salience 7 when $b : E2(ts == 0) P() then delete($b); end
rule R6 salience 6 when P() then end
rule RL salience 1 when P() then end
_finding: FLAG-EAGER ARC original witness (born as the halt arc's voided hm2 cell). Oracle 3x [TJ1, R6, TJ1, RL] (K never activates — flag-eager refuses the fresh-P x flagged-E2(0) pair). Engine post-D-178: [TJ1, K, RN, R6, TJ1, RL] — K fires (the defect), and RN follows correctly per the landing law once K's RHS delete lands (the pre-D-178 recorded sequence lacked RN; refreshed at D-179). MINIMAL witness: fe2_minimal (2 types, 1 rule). MECHANISM (D-179, trace-pinned): the plain-join insert walks corpse-check only the PARTNER side (right walk: l.iter().any(is_expired) on left partners, phreak.rs ~1930; left walk: is_expired(f) on right partners, ~1954) — the WALKING fact/tuple is never flag-checked. A corpse whose own memory-fold is deferred past its flagging — unlinked-path staging accumulation (D-101/fz_7_145) or the flush right-stash (D-102) — walks in flagged and forms pairs. The temporal arm does NOT share the blind spot (fe7 green). Fix shape (NOT ported, Bryan gate): corpse-guard the partner LOOP of both plain walks (memory push stays — flag-eager, retraction-lazy); check the AB-arm sites (~595/612, same partner-only pattern, no witness yet) at port time.
